description: LPETG — the canonical sortase A (SrtA) recognition motif (a single instance of the Gram-positive LPXTG cell-wall sorting signal); SrtA cleaves between the Thr and Gly (LPET|G), forming an acyl-enzyme thioester that is resolved by transpeptidation onto an N-terminal oligoglycine acceptor, making LPETG the standard C-terminal handle for site-specific protein ligation (sortagging) and conjugation.

references.authors: Mazmanian SK, Liu G, Ton-That H, Schneewind O
references.title: Staphylococcus aureus sortase, an enzyme that anchors surface proteins to the cell wall

references.authors: Ton-That H, Liu G, Mazmanian SK, Faull KF, Schneewind O
references.title: Purification and characterization of sortase, the transpeptidase that cleaves surface proteins of Staphylococcus aureus at the LPXTG motif

references.authors: Mao H, Hart SA, Schink A, Pollok BA
references.title: Sortase-mediated protein ligation: a new method for protein engineering

references.authors: Guimaraes CP, Witte MD, Theile CS, Bozkurt G, Kundrat L, Blom AEM, Ploegh HL
references.title: Site-specific C-terminal and internal loop labeling of proteins using sortase-mediated reactions

references.authors: Chen I, Dorr BM, Liu DR
references.title: A general strategy for the evolution of bond-forming enzymes using yeast display

references.authors: Dorr BM, Ham HO, An C, Chaikof EL, Liu DR
references.title: Reprogramming the specificity of sortase enzymes

references.authors: Hansenova Manaskova S, Nazmi K, van Belkum A, Bikker FJ, van Wamel WJB, Veerman ECI
references.title: Synthetic LPETG-Containing Peptide Incorporation in the Staphylococcus aureus Cell-Wall in a Sortase A- and Growth Phase-Dependent Manner

references.authors: O'Halloran DP, Wynne K, Geoghegan JA
references.title: Protein A is released into the Staphylococcus aureus culture supernatant with an unprocessed sorting signal

label: LPETG_tag
note: Sortase A (SrtA) recognition motif and a single instance of the Gram-positive LPXTG cell-wall sorting signal (Leu-Pro-Glu-Thr-Gly). SrtA cleaves the scissile peptide bond between the P1 Thr and the P1' Gly (LPET|G), forming a covalent acyl-enzyme thioester intermediate at the active-site Cys; this intermediate is resolved by nucleophilic attack of an N-terminal oligoglycine (Gly-n, typically GGG/GGGGG) acceptor, ligating the upstream protein to the acceptor and regenerating an LPETGG... junction. Whole-part feature spanning all 5 residues; this is a single atomic functional element with no functional sub-features. The X position of the LPXTG consensus is most commonly Glu (E) in efficient engineering substrates. Source-verified to UniProt P02976 (Protein A) Motif 'LPXTG sorting signal' at positions 482-486, 5/5 identity.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt P02976 (SPA_STAA8, Immunoglobulin G-binding protein A, S. aureus NCTC 8325, Swiss-Prot PE=1 reviewed), positions 482-486 (1-based), explicit Motif annotation 'LPXTG sorting signal'; sequence LPETG independently fetched and confirmed exact 5/5 match

functional_claims.label: LPETG is an instance of the LPXTG sortase recognition motif; sortase A cleaves between the Thr and the Gly (LPET|G)

value.scissile_bond: between P1 Thr and P1' Gly (LPET|G)
value.x_position_note: Glu (E) is commonly found at the X position of efficient natural and engineering SrtA substrates

source.quote: Cleavage between the threonine and the glycine of the LPXTG motif liberates the carboxyl of threonine to form an amide bond with the amino group of the pentaglycine crossbridge (2, 3), thereby tethering the C terminus end of surface proteins to the bacterial peptidoglycan (4–7). [Lines 25-28]. Additionally: Staphylococcal extracts, as well as purified sortase, catalyze the hydroxylaminolysis of peptides bearing an LPXTG motif [lines 14-15]. And: Purified SrtADN was incubated with the LPXTG peptide Dabcyl-QALPETGEE-Edans, and cleavage was monitored [lines 179-188], demonstrating LPETG as an active substrate for SrtA-mediated cleavage.

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Directly actionable for designers: specifies exact enzyme (SrtA), recognition sequence (LPETG), and scissile bond position (Thr|Gly). This information discriminates LPETG from other tags and determines whether a protein construct will be efficiently processed.

functional_claims.label: Sortase A is a transpeptidase: it cleaves LPXTG to form an acyl-enzyme thioester at the active-site Cys, then transfers the acyl group to an N-terminal oligoglycine acceptor (transpeptidation)

value.step_1: cleavage of the Thr-Gly bond of LPXTG and formation of a covalent acyl-enzyme thioester intermediate at the active-site cysteine
value.step_2: resolution of the thioester by nucleophilic attack of an N-terminal oligoglycine (Gly-n) amine, ligating the LPET-acyl group to the acceptor
value.net_result: site-specific ligation of an LPXTG-tagged protein to a Gly-n-bearing partner, reconstituting an LPETGG... junction
value.natural_function: anchoring surface proteins to the Gram-positive cell-wall peptidoglycan (via the lipid II / pentaglycine cross-bridge as natural acceptor)

source.quote: Protein A is cleaved by a transpeptidase, sortase, between the threonine and the glycine of a conserved LPXTG motif (5). The carboxyl group of threonine is amide-linked to the amino group of the pentaglycine cross-bridge, thereby tethering the COOH-terminal end of protein A to the bacterial cell wall (6). [Lines 211-217]. The srtA gene specifies a protein of 206 amino acids with a potential NH2-terminal signal peptide/membrane anchor sequence and a presumed active-site cysteine at position 184, consistent with the observation that the cell wall sorting reaction is sensitive to reagents that modify sulfhydryl groups. [Lines 418-424]. Together, our data reveal that srtA encodes sortase, the transpeptidase that anchors surface proteins to the bacterial cell wall (24). [Lines 450-452]

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Explains the molecular mechanism of sortase-mediated ligation, critical for understanding how LPETG functions operationally and how it differs from passive affinity/detection tags; directly guides use (cysteine-dependent, requires oligoglycine acceptor, results in covalent ligation)

functional_claims.label: LPETG is the native LPXTG sorting signal of S. aureus Protein A (SpA); native SpA carries the exact LPETG sequence in its precursor

value.origin_protein: Immunoglobulin G-binding protein A (Protein A / SpA), Staphylococcus aureus (UniProt P02976)
value.also_present_in: FnbA (UniProt P14738) at positions 982-986, and many other Gram-positive surface proteins as the conserved LPXTG class signal
value.biological_context: C-terminal cell-wall sorting signal directing SrtA-mediated covalent anchoring to peptidoglycan

source.quote: Protein A is released into the Staphylococcus aureus culture supernatant with an unprocessed sorting signal

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Tautological — restates that the part IS the native LPXTG sorting signal from S. aureus Protein A, a fact already evident from the part's name and description; provides no actionable design information, comparative discrimination, or failure-mode knowledge that an engineer would consult when choosing or operating this part.
functional_claims.comment: superseded by natural_origin__v2: The asserted residue positions (482-486) are factually incorrect per the primary source, which states the sorting signal spans residues 474-508 with the LPETG motif at positions 470-474. The origin claim itself (that LPETG is native to SpA) is supported, but the positional coordinates must be corrected.

functional_claims.label: LPETG is the standard C-terminal handle for sortase-mediated protein ligation (sortagging): a protein carrying C-terminal LPETG can be site-specifically conjugated to molecules bearing an N-terminal glycine

value.handle: C-terminal LPETG on the protein of interest
value.acceptor: N-terminal (oligo)glycine on the partner (peptide, protein, small molecule, surface, D-peptide, or branched peptide)
uses: C-terminal and internal-loop labeling of proteins
value.founding_in_vitro_demonstration: use of LPETG for in vitro protein engineering / conjugation to (D)-peptides, branched peptides, and small molecules bearing an N-terminal glycine

source.quote: Sortase-mediated protein ligation: a new method for protein engineering

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Specifies the operational method (sortase-mediated ligation), the binding partners (C-terminal LPETG + N-terminal Gly), and concrete applications (bioconjugation, immobilization, labeling); enables direct selection and use of this part over alternatives

functional_claims.label: LPETG must be placed at (or near) the C-terminus of the protein of interest and in an accessible, unstructured region; the Gly that follows it is consumed/relocated by the ligation

value.position: C-terminal of the protein of interest (commonly POI-LPETG-His6 so an affinity tag downstream is removed upon ligation)
value.structural_requirement: the LPXTG motif should lie in a flexible, solvent-accessible / unstructured region for efficient cleavage
value.junction_after_ligation: LPETGG... (the acceptor's N-terminal Gly residues become part of the product)

source.quote: The sortase-recognition sequence is usually engineered at the C terminus of the protein to be modified, with the G or A residue in amide linkage, followed by an affinity purification handle (e.g., His6) that is lost upon reaction. The efficiency of the sortase-mediated reaction depends on the flexibility and accessibility of the region comprising the sortase A-recognition motif. As long as the LPXTG/A motif is introduced in an unstructured segment of the substrate protein, sortase can recognize the sequence. An LPXTG motif that is highly structured is usually a poor sortase substrate.

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Actionable constraints on C-terminal placement, accessibility requirements, linker design strategy, and junction composition are essential design parameters for successful sortagging applications

functional_claims.label: Host-agnostic sequence element: LPETG is a short peptide motif and the sortase enzyme is supplied separately, so the handle functions independently of the expression host

value.element_type: sortase recognition / cell-wall sorting signal (peptide motif)
value.host_dependence: none for the motif itself; ligation requires sortase A supplied in vitro (or co-expressed in vivo)
value.enzyme_origin: Staphylococcus aureus sortase A (SrtA); commonly the evolved pentamutant eSrtA for enhanced activity

source.quote: Sortase (SrtA), a transpeptidase from Staphylococcus aureus, catalyzes a cell-wall sorting reaction at an LPXTG motif by cleaving between threonine and glycine and subsequently joining the carboxyl group of threonine to an amino group of pentaglycine. We have applied this transpeptidyl activity of sortase to in vitro protein ligation. We found that in the presence of sortase, protein/peptide with an LPXTG motif can be specifically ligated to an aminoglycine protein/peptide via an amide bond. Additionally, sortase can even conjugate substrates such as (d)-peptides, synthetic branched peptides, and aminoglycine-derivatized small molecules to the C terminus of a recombinant protein.

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Datasheet_slot claim: directly influences designer choice of expression system; explains why any organism/context works (motif is sequence + separate enzyme)

functional_claims.label: The catalyst, not the motif, is the main engineered variant: the evolved SrtA pentamutant (eSrtA) has greatly improved activity toward LPETG; orthogonal sortases instead recognize altered motifs (LAXTG / LPXSG) rather than LPETG

value.motif_variants: LPXTG class; X is most often Glu in efficient substrates (LPETG); other X residues occur in natural signals
value.evolved_catalyst: eSrtA pentamutant (P94R/D160N/D165A/K190E/K196T), reported with markedly improved activity toward LPETG
value.reported_improvement: ~140-fold increased activity vs wild-type SrtA (quantitative value from a primary text not directly accessed)
value.orthogonal_motifs: engineered sortases reprogrammed to recognize LAXTG and LPXSG, independent of canonical LPETG, enabling dual labeling

source.quote: A general strategy for the evolution of bond-forming enzymes using yeast display

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Discriminates LPETG from engineered variants, provides quantitative enzyme performance data (fold activity improvement), and specifies which evolved sortase (pentamutant) to use — directly operational for a designer choosing between WT and evolved SrtA.
functional_claims.comment: The "orthogonal_motifs" clause (LAXTG/LPXSG recognition by engineered sortases) is not discussed in the cited paper (Chen et al. 2011); it appears to conflate two separate publications. The pentamutant's actual 120-fold activity (not 140-fold) is documented in Table 1 line 791 of Chen et al.

functional_claims.label: LPETG is the native LPXTG sorting signal of S. aureus Protein A (SpA); native SpA carries the exact LPETG sequence in its precursor

value.origin_protein: Immunoglobulin G-binding protein A (Protein A / SpA), Staphylococcus aureus (UniProt P02976)
value.residues: 470-474 (LPETG), or more broadly 474-508 for the complete sorting signal
value.also_present_in: FnbA (UniProt P14738) at positions 982-986, and many other Gram-positive surface proteins as the conserved LPXTG class signal
value.biological_context: C-terminal cell-wall sorting signal directing SrtA-mediated covalent anchoring to peptidoglycan

source.quote: The paper states: "DNA encoding the SpA sorting signal (residues 474 to 508)" and identifies the LPETG motif in peptides at positions 470-484 (Table 2, peptide "KAQALPETGEENPFI").

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Tautological — restates that the part IS the native LPXTG sorting signal from S. aureus Protein A, a fact already evident from the part's name and description; provides no actionable design information, comparative discrimination, or failure-mode knowledge that an engineer would consult when choosing or operating this part.
